text.title: Structured Output — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules: provider-native first, schema-validated parse, repair-once retry, raw logging on failure, no recursive schemas.

rule.statement: When the chosen provider supports structured output natively (response_format, tool-use JSON, schema response), it MUST be used. Manual JSON-prompting on a structured-output-capable provider is forbidden.

rationale.source: OpenAI Structured Outputs docs 2024; Anthropic JSON-mode benchmarks

rule.rationale: Provider-side constrained decoding eliminates the parse-failure class. Manual prompt-engineered JSON has 5-15% parse failure rate even on capable models; constrained decoding drops it to <0.1%.

rule.statement: Output MUST be parsed via the same Pydantic / JSON Schema used to constrain the model. Accepting a model response without validation is forbidden; mismatched types between schema and parsed value MUST raise.
rule.rationale: Provider may return JSON-shaped output that does not match the schema (constrained decoding has edge cases). Always validate; never trust the model's compliance claim.

rule.statement: On parse failure, the system MUST retry exactly once with the schema + parse error included in the prompt. Further retries are forbidden — they amplify cost without quality lift.
rule.rationale: Infinite retry loops on schema failures burn budget. Repair-once succeeds on ≈90% of remaining cases; further attempts have diminishing return.

rule.statement: When parse fails twice, the raw model output AND the schema MUST be logged to error sink for investigation. Returning a typed-zero / None silently is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent fallback hides systematic schema-drift bugs. Logged raw output lets engineers refine schema or detect model regression.

rule.statement: Schemas MUST NOT contain recursive type references or unbounded depth. Convert recursive structures to flat arrays with parent-id references.
rule.rationale: Provider constrained decoding does not support recursive schemas; the call fails at the API layer. Flattening preserves structure while remaining tractable.
